differential_diagnoses: Coxiella burnetii infection (Q fever endocarditis or myocarditis)
Drug-induced liver injury secondary to supplements
next_steps: Perform a transthoracic echocardiogram to assess for valvular vegetations and wall motion abnormalities
Obtain a cardiac MRI to evaluate for late gadolinium enhancement suggestive of myocarditis or sarcoidosis
Send comprehensive infectious serologies including Coxiella burnetii (Phase I and II titers), Borrelia burgdorferi (repeat with Western blot), and Bartonella species
Order a comprehensive autoimmune and inflammatory panel (ACE level, ANA, ANCA)
Perform 24-48 hour ambulatory cardiac rhythm monitoring (Holter) to characterize the irregular rhythm and conduction blocks
Avoid AV-nodal blocking agents until structural and infectious etiologies are excluded
